steps: Go to https://hub.docker.com and log in or create a Docker account
Click on your avatar in the top-right corner and select 'Account Settings'
Navigate to 'Personal access tokens' (or go directly to https://hub.docker.com/settings/security)
Enter a description and choose the access permissions (Read, Read/Write, or Read/Write/Delete)
Click 'Generate' and copy the token — it will not be shown again

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Personal Access Token
credential_fields.description: Your Docker Hub personal access token. Used as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

notes: Personal access tokens are prefixed with 'dckr_pat_'
Token is shown only once at creation — copy it immediately
Free Docker accounts have rate limits on Docker Hub pulls (100 pulls per 6 hours for anonymous, 200 for free authenticated accounts)
Tokens can be scoped to Read-only, Read/Write, or Read/Write/Delete
Docker Hub API base URL is https://hub.docker.com/v2 (not api.docker.com for most endpoints)

test_endpoint.description: Retrieve authenticated user information (replace {username} with your Docker Hub username)
